quiz.title: Unit 7 Quiz 3, Grade 11 Biology
quiz.subtitle: Cell Division: Mitosis Stages and Cytokinesis
quiz.subject: Biology
quiz.topic: Prophase, Metaphase, Anaphase, Telophase, Spindle Fibers, Kinetochore, Cytokinesis in Animal vs Plant Cells

questions.question: Which of the following is the first stage of mitosis?
questions.explanation: Prophase is the first stage of mitosis. During prophase, chromosomes condense, the nuclear envelope breaks down, and the spindle apparatus begins to form.

questions.question: During which stage of mitosis do chromosomes become visible under a light microscope?
questions.explanation: Chromosomes become visible during prophase as they condense into discrete structures. During interphase, chromatin is uncoiled and not visible under light microscopy.

questions.question: During metaphase, chromosomes align at the:
questions.explanation: During metaphase, chromosomes line up at the equatorial plane (metaphase plate) with spindle fibers attached to their kinetochores, ensuring proper separation in anaphase.

questions.question: Which of the following occurs during anaphase?
options: Chromosomes line up at the equator
questions.explanation: Anaphase is characterized by the separation of sister chromatids, which are pulled by spindle fibers to opposite poles of the cell.

questions.question: Telophase is characterized by:
options: Reformation of nuclear envelope and nucleolus
Chromosomes lining up at the metaphase plate
questions.explanation: During telophase, the nuclear envelope reforms around each set of chromosomes, the nucleolus reappears, and chromosomes begin to decondense.

questions.question: Which of the following proteins regulates progression through mitosis?
questions.explanation: Cyclins are regulatory proteins that control the cell cycle by binding to cyclin-dependent kinases (CDKs). Their concentrations rise and fall throughout the cell cycle, helping to trigger key events like the transition from G2 to M phase. Hemoglobin carries oxygen, keratin is a structural protein in skin/hair, and myosin is involved in muscle contraction.

questions.question: Cytokinesis usually occurs:
questions.explanation: Cytokinesis (division of the cytoplasm) typically occurs after telophase, completing the cell division process and producing two daughter cells.

questions.question: Which of the following is true about chromosomes during mitosis?
questions.explanation: During mitosis, chromosomes are already duplicated (having undergone DNA replication in S phase), consisting of two sister chromatids joined at the centromere.

questions.question: Which of these events occurs only in mitosis and not in interphase?
questions.explanation: Chromosome condensation occurs only during mitosis (prophase). DNA replication occurs in S phase (interphase), and protein synthesis/metabolic activity occur throughout interphase.

questions.question: The mitotic spindle is made of:
questions.explanation: The mitotic spindle is composed of microtubules, which are protein filaments that extend from centrosomes and attach to chromosomes to guide their movement.

questions.question: What ensures that sister chromatids move to opposite poles?
questions.explanation: Kinetochore microtubules attach to the kinetochore on each sister chromatid and shorten during anaphase, pulling the chromatids to opposite poles.

questions.question: Which stage is sometimes called the 'resting phase' but is actually very active metabolically?
questions.explanation: Interphase is sometimes misleadingly called the 'resting phase,' but it is highly active with DNA replication, protein synthesis, and cell growth occurring.

questions.question: How many daughter cells are produced at the end of mitosis in animals?
questions.explanation: Mitosis produces two genetically identical daughter cells from one parent cell. (Meiosis produces four daughter cells.)

questions.question: Select all that apply: The mitotic phase (M phase) includes:
questions.explanation: The M phase (mitotic phase) includes both mitosis (nuclear division) and cytokinesis (cytoplasmic division). DNA replication occurs during S phase of interphase, and interphase itself is the period between divisions, not part of M phase.

questions.question: In plant cells, cytokinesis differs from animal cells because:
questions.explanation: In plant cells, cytokinesis occurs through the formation of a cell plate from Golgi-derived vesicles, which develops into a new cell wall. Animal cells form a cleavage furrow.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered the stages of mitosis and cytokinesis! 🔬
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review what happens in each phase of mitosis.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on the order of mitotic events and key structures.
scoreMessages.0: Please review prophase, metaphase, anaphase, telophase, and cytokinesis.
